This is very important. This course is on consistency. Consistency, ladies and gentlemen, is the number one attribute that you are going to need to be successful. Because repeating successful actions over and over and over again, progress and not perfection, growth and not being in a resuscitative state, flourishing and prospering instead of being in that mode where everything is just inward. You need to maintain a consistency in the knowledge in which you can apply inside of this program. You need a consistency in the information that comes in around you that gives you these kinds of insights and lifts you up. You need a consistency in the environment that you need to maintain in order to stay affluent. You have to have a consistency in your daily actions for people to perceive you in a growth mindset. You need to grow yourself. You need to be in a state where you are consistently looking at what you can do to flourish, prosper, profit and prosper and just overall grow. Most people in life, they get in these states. Some people, in no offense to Tony Robbins, he's one of the only reasons that I'm here right now presenting this to you. I give massive credit to Tony Robbins. One of the one things that Tony Robbins unfortunately doesn't allow for in a sense from his programs is he doesn't allow you to maintain the state that he puts you in. He gets you all kinds of hype. He gets you motivated. He gets you pumped. He gets you in a position of power. But then people don't know how to maintain that because they go into such a high state and they don't know how to maintain that height. They aren't growing on an incrementally healthy level. They're going straight up. It is tough to climb a hill. It is tough to climb a cliff straight up. It is tough to climb a vertical. It's easier to walk on a small incline. It's easier to jog up a small incline. It is tough once again to sprint up a cliff your entire life. It's not how it works. You're not going to be able to maintain that. That's a push, not a pull. So one thing that I want to teach you about inconsistency is called the pull system. Okay? There's these images, these gifts, these videos, these articles, these people that are going to talk to you about things in life that you have to kind of like move things forward and you have to push things. And that, you know, sometimes in life you feel that there's a hill and that you're moving up and you're down here at the bottom and you feel like you're pushing something up the hill. You feel like you are trying to get this thing up the hill and you're having to just push it. It's tough and it's a struggle. I remember to the T when I was sitting on a suitcase where they shoot 48 hours at Miami saying to myself that I was almost at the top of the hill. I remember that where I felt like I literally was pushing something. I literally remember this mentality and then there was this shift that occurred. Now some people in life when they get to the top of the hill, they like literally make this judgment that things shifted, right? They make an opinion, a.k.a. they draw a conclusion and then have a behavior that's dictated off the conclusion that they've now made a shift. I remember the day when I told myself I've reached critical mass and I am now, everything's smooth. Everything's moving quick. I didn't have to push things anymore. I remember that shift. But guess what, ladies and gentlemen? It's just a shift in your mentality. This right here, this is not real. There's no hill you're climbing up. There's nothing in which you're pushing up a hill. That is not real. You have no data. You have no reality to create all of this. That is bullshit. And you telling yourself this is putting you in this state where you're pushing something up a hill. Why not create something that allows you to maintain consistency and successful actions by telling yourself that the other side is where you're at and that things are easy and that you are in a model where you are being pulled down the hill? I like to think personally that I have this massive ball that's just stumbling down the hill and I'm hanging on and I'm not barely hanging on. I'm fucking wrapped around this thing. I'm hanging on to this. I'm riding down the hill and the ball is rolling. I don't think I'm pushing anything. I think I've grabbed onto something and I think I'm rolling down the hill now. That's my mentality. That's how I move through life. That enables me to have a consistency in actions. It is 10, 15 PM. What day is it? 25th. It's the 24th day. Tuesday, Wednesday. It's Wednesday right now and right now at 10, 15 some people are asleep. I'm right here right now at the same level of energy, a consistency in actions, traveling coast to coast and under a week because I feel like I'm grabbed onto something that's moving so fast and it's enabling me to move that fast. You see what I'm saying? You need to have the mentality that you're rolling down the hill. You don't need to have the mentality that you're pushing something up. You need to create a pull for yourself. Something that literally can be perceived like you have to do it because you're pulled, you're drawn to it. There's something that enables you to look at what's going on in your life and tell yourself. You can self-assess this. Are you having to move things forward or are things pulling you to them? Do you have that girl who's pulling you towards her like a magnet, like a strong force of gravity? Do you have a business that's pulling you to take massive action every single day? Do you have a lifestyle that pulls you to wake up and get out of bed and maintain living that lifestyle or do you live something mundane where you're having to push and push and push and push? It's the key to consistency. Create a pull for yourself instead of a push that way you can profit and press.
